title: CIL Geology — Advanced Mock 13 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness

questions.solution: All of the above

questions.solution: August

questions.stem: Ayushman Bharat PM-JAY provides health cover of how much per family?
questions.solution: ₹5 lakh

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: IMF World Economic Outlook 2026 projected India's growth at:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Indus Valley Civilization belonged to which age?
questions.solution: Bronze Age

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The 2026 ICC Cricket World Cup was hosted by:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The new Chairman of Coal India Limited appointed in 2026 was:
questions.solution: A.K. Jha

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Consumer Price Index (CPI) measures:

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who wrote 'India's Ancient Past'?
questions.solution: R.S. Sharma

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Deccan Plateau is primarily composed of:
questions.solution: Basalt

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The 2026 National Film Award for Best Feature Film was:
questions.solution: Aattam

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Coal & Mining in India
questions.stem: What does ROM coal stand for?
questions.solution: Run of Mine

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by what percentage YoY?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The Permanent Settlement of Bengal was introduced by:
questions.solution: Lord Cornwallis

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Fiscal deficit is:
options: Total expenditure minus total receipts excluding borrowings
questions.solution: Total expenditure minus total receipts excluding borrowings

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Coal & Mining in India
questions.stem: CIL headquarters is located in which city?
questions.solution: Kolkata

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Ajanta and Ellora caves are located in:
questions.solution: Maharashtra

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.topic: Static GK · Coal & Mining in India
questions.stem: Coal beneficiation is done primarily to:
questions.solution: Reduce ash content

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A pipe fills tank in 8 h, another in 10 h. A third empties in 6 h. First two opened for 2 h, then third opened. Time to fill:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A boat covers 40 km downstream and 24 km upstream in 8 h. 32 km downstream and 40 km upstream in 10 h. Still water speed:
questions.solution: 12 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An amount at 8% CI becomes ₹7,776 in 2 years. Its value 3 years ago was:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A and B fill together in 8 min. A alone takes 12 min. After 3 min, B leaks at half rate. Total time:
questions.solution: 10.5 min

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 200 m and 150 m run at 72 km/h and 54 km/h opposite. Time to cross:
questions.solution: 10 sec

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In a cylinder, radius is halved and height doubled. Volume change:
questions.solution: 50% decrease

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If each observation is doubled, the standard deviation:
questions.solution: Doubles

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The sum of first 15 odd natural numbers is:

questions.stem: A father is three times as old as his son. In 12 years, he will be twice as old. The son's present age:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A card is drawn from 52. Probability it's a king or red:

questions.stem: The average score of a batsman in 20 innings is 32. How many must he score in next 10 to raise average to 40?

questions.stem: A vessel has 60 L milk. 12 L removed and replaced with water. Milk in final mixture:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Pipe A fills in 12 h, B in 15 h. They work alternately starting with A. When full?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Find the largest number of 4 digits divisible by 12, 15, 20, and 35:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Two trains 160 m and 140 m run at same speed on parallel tracks. Time to overtake is 30 sec. Speed:
questions.solution: 36 km/h

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 6 men or 8 women or 12 children can do in 9 days, 3 men + 4 women + 6 children in:
questions.solution: 6 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum is divided among A, B, C, D in ratio 2:3:5:7. If B's share is ₹1,200, D's share:

questions.stem: A speaks truth 80%, B 70%. Probability they contradict on a fact:

questions.stem: A sum triples in 12 years at SI. Rate:

questions.stem: A bag has 5 red, 4 blue, 3 green balls. Two drawn without replacement. Probability both red:

questions.stem: If p:q = 3:4, q:r = 2:3, r:s = 3:5, then p:s =

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A rectangular park 60 m × 40 m has a 5 m path around it. Path area:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 15 days. B is 25% more efficient. B alone takes:
questions.solution: 12 days

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: In an election, 5% invalid. Winner got 60% valid votes and won by 6,840. Total votes:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Series: |, ||, |||, ||||, ? (tally marks)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All rings are bells. Some bells are chains. Conclusions: I. Some rings are chains. II. All bells are rings.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a dice, faces are 1 to 6. If 3 is at top, 5 at front, what is at bottom in standard dice?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: 3, 7, 16, 35, 74, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: AZ, BY, CX, DW, ?
questions.solution: EV

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these looks identical in a vertical mirror? H, O, X, N
questions.solution: H, O, X

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Alternating: White, Black, White, Black, ?
questions.solution: White

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All squares are rectangles. All rectangles are parallelograms. No parallelogram is a triangle. Conclusions: I. No square is a triangle. II. Some rectangles are not triangles.
questions.solution: Both follow

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a dice, if 2 is on top and 3 on left, what is on right if standard?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the wrong number: 3, 6, 15, 45, 157.5, 630

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: JK, LM, NO, PQ, ?
questions.solution: RS

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Mirror image of 9:10 in a vertical mirror:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Rotating arrow: →, ↘, ↓, ↙, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some books are interesting. All interesting things are engaging. Conclusions: I. Some books are engaging. II. All engaging things are interesting.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Four positions of dice show: 5-1-2, 6-1-4, 3-2-1, 6-5-3. Which is opposite 1?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: 0, 6, 24, 60, 120, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, SACHIN = 58, DRAVID = 49, then GANGULY = ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 1 January 2026 is Thursday, how many Sundays in January?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A walks 25 m north, 15 m east, 25 m south. Another man B walks 15 m west from same start. B is in which direction of A?
questions.solution: West

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A's mother is the sister of B's father. How is A related to B?
questions.solution: Cousin

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A,B,C,D are in circle. A is 2nd left of C. B is opposite A. D is left of B. Who is right of C?
questions.solution: D

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: 1, 2, 5, 14, 41, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All A are B. Some B are C. All C are D. No D is E. Conclusions: I. No A is E. II. Some C are not E.
questions.solution: Both follow

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He told the news to his brother and I.
options: He told the news to I and my brother.
He told the news to his brother and me.
He told the news to his brother and I.
He told the news to my brother and myself.
questions.solution: He told the news to his brother and me.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She ____ a keen interest in geology.
questions.solution: showed

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The informations provided is accurate.
options: The information provided has been accurate.
questions.solution: The information provided is accurate.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The accident ____ a review of safety protocols.
questions.solution: prompted

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The team is working on the project since May.
options: The team had been working on the project since May.
The team was working on the project since May.
The team has worked on the project since May.
The team has been working on the project since May.
questions.solution: The team has been working on the project since May.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government should ____ heavy penalties for violations.
questions.solution: impose

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I prefer coffee than tea.
questions.solution: I prefer coffee to tea.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The project was ____ due to lack of funds.
questions.solution: abandoned

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is smarter than any student in the class.
options: She is the smartest than any student in the class.
She is smarter than any other student in the class.
She is smarter than any student in the class.
She is smarter than all student in the class.
questions.solution: She is smarter than any other student in the class.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She is ____ for her contribution to science.
questions.solution: renowned

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Hardly had he arrived than the meeting started.
options: He had hardly arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived then the meeting started.
Hardly he had arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.
questions.solution: Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The report ____ serious concerns about safety.
questions.solution: raises

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I have been working here from 2015.
options: I have been working here since 2015.
I have been working here from the year 2015.
I have been working here from 2015.
questions.solution: I have been working here since 2015.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The government plans to ____ the mining policy.
questions.solution: revise

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: I am looking forward to meet you.
options: I am looking forward for meeting you.
I look forward to meet you.
I am looking forward to meeting you.
I am looking forward to meet you.
questions.solution: I am looking forward to meeting you.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The new method is more ____ than the old one.
questions.solution: efficient

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is better than any student in the class.
options: He is best than any student in the class.
He is better than any other student in the class.
He is the best than any student in the class.
He is better than any student in the class.
questions.solution: He is better than any other student in the class.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: She is (A) / more taller (B) / than her (C) / younger sister. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'taller'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: digital / has / way / the / changed / communication / we
options: Communication digital has changed the way we
We communicate way the has changed digital technology
Digital technology has changed the way we communicate
The way we communicate has changed digital technology
questions.solution: Digital technology has changed the way we communicate

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of OBSTINATE:
questions.solution: Stubborn

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: She is (A) / one of the (B) / most talented (C) / singer in the industry. (D)
questions.solution: D - should be 'singers'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of DISPARITY:
questions.solution: Inequality

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: One of the miners (A) / have been (B) / hospitalised after (C) / the accident. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'has been'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of DISPARITY:
questions.solution: Parity

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of CATALYST:
questions.solution: Trigger

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnesium skarn deposits:
options: Mg-Ti-Fe skarn from dolomite + granitic intrusions. Phlogopite, forsterite, spinel
questions.solution: Skarn: Ca (calcic) or Mg (magnesian). W, Cu, Fe, Zn. Pyroxene + garnet + wollastonite.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: K-Ar dating requires:
options: Measurement of K and Ar independently
questions.solution: K-Ar: 40K → 40Ar. Measure K by flame photometry/ICP, Ar by mass spectrometry. Ar must be trapped (closure temperature).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentary structures — load cast:
options: Bulbous projections on bottom of bed. Soft sediment deformation
questions.solution: Load cast: differential loading. Flute cast: current scour. Both sole marks (bottom of bed).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Kink band indicates:
questions.solution: Kink band: sharp fold with straight limbs. Indicates simple shear. Common in foliated rocks with strong planar fabric.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lamproite:
questions.solution: Lamproite: K-rich, Mg-rich. Phlogopite + olivine + K-richerite. Source rock for diamonds.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Symplectite:
options: Intergrowth of two minerals (e.g., Cpx + Pl) replacing higher-P assemblage. Opx + Cpx + Pl from Grt breakdown
questions.solution: Symplectite: vermicular texture. Decompression reaction. Fingerprint of rapid exhumation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: GIS data models — raster:
options: Grid cells. Continuous data (elevation, rainfall). Cell size = resolution
questions.solution: Raster: simple analysis, large storage. Compression: run-length, LZW, JPEG2000.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Condensed section:
options: Slow deposition (pelagic/hemipelagic) during maximum flooding. High organic matter. Downlap surface
questions.solution: Condensed: glauconite, phosphorite, organic-rich. Maximum flooding surface (MFS).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mica is well known for its perfect:
options: Basal cleavage (it splits into thin sheets)
questions.solution: Mica has one perfect cleavage direction, peeling into thin elastic sheets.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A volcanic rock containing many gas-bubble cavities is described as:
questions.solution: Vesicles are voids left by escaping gas in rapidly cooled lava (e.g. scoria, pumice).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coal rank with the highest fixed-carbon content and calorific value is:
questions.solution: Anthracite is the highest-rank coal, with the greatest carbon content and heating value.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rift zone: continental rifting leads to:
options: Passive margin (if successful) or failed rift (aulacogen)
questions.solution: Rift: East African (active), Atlantic (passive margins). Aulacogen: failed arm of triple junction.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Mantle convection drives plate motion through:
options: Basal drag and ridge push forces
questions.solution: Slab pull + ridge push + basal drag. Plates move cm/year.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mass extinction events: five major:
questions.solution: P-T extinction: 95% marine species. K-Pg: dinosaurs. End-Ordovician: glaciation.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Pangaea supercontinent existed during:
questions.solution: Pangaea: assembled Carboniferous-Permian, broke up Jurassic-Cretaceous. Caused significant climate and biogeographic effects.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Residual deposits — lateritic bauxite:
options: Al(OH)₃ (gibbsite, boehmite, diaspore). Intense lateritic weathering of aluminosilicate rocks
questions.solution: Bauxite: Al ore. Weipa (Australia), Panchpatmali (India). Laterite caps.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: U-Pb concordia diagram shows:
questions.solution: Concordia: 206Pb/238U vs 207Pb/235U. Discordant points indicate lead loss. Upper intercept = crystallization age.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic reaction textures: coronas (symplectites):
options: Rim minerals around reactant mineral, formed during incomplete reaction
questions.solution: Corona: olivine rimmed by Opx+Spinel. Symplectite: vermicular intergrowth. Records reaction progress.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Chlorite grade metamorphism indicates:
questions.solution: Chlorite: index mineral for greenschist facies. ~300-450°C, moderate pressure. Typical of regional metamorphism.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Laterite formation:
options: Intense chemical weathering under tropical monsoon. Fe/Al enrichment. Bauxite
questions.solution: Laterite: duricrust. Age: Tertiary-Quaternary. Transition: laterite → bauxite (Al-rich).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Bajada is:
options: Coalescing alluvial fans at mountain base
questions.solution: Bajada: series of overlapping alluvial fans forming apron at mountain front. Common in arid regions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Site investigation methods:
options: Desk study → field mapping → geophysics → drilling → in-situ testing
questions.solution: Phased approach: each stage informs next. Minimize cost, maximize information.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quartz c-axis preferred orientation:
options: Crystallographic fabric by dynamic recrystallization. Type I (basal slip) vs Type II (prism slip) cross-girdles
questions.solution: Quartz CPO: fabric temperature-dependent. Low T: basal <a>. High T: prism <a>.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Graptolite biostratigraphy:
options: Ordovician-Silurian. Planktonic colonial hemichordates. Dendroid (benthic) vs graptoloid (planktonic)
questions.solution: Graptolites: organic (periderm). Didymograptus (L. Ordovician), Monograptus (Silurian). Extinct Devonian.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chronostratigraphy:
options: Time-rock units (System, Series, Stage). Based on absolute or relative age
questions.solution: Chronostratigraphic: Cretaceous System, Oxfordian Stage. Geochronologic: Cretaceous Period.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Weathering types:
options: Physical (freeze-thaw, exfoliation, salt crystal growth) and chemical (dissolution, oxidation, hydrolysis)
questions.solution: Physical: mechanical breakup. Chemical: mineral alteration. Spheroidal weathering of jointed rock.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stereographic projection:
options: Tool for structural data analysis (poles to planes, fold axes)
questions.solution: Stereonet: lower hemisphere equal-area (Schmidt) or equal-angle (Wulff).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical conductivity of mantle:
options: Increases with depth. Asthenosphere: higher conductivity (partial melt)
questions.solution: Mantle conductivity: olivine + melt. High conductivity zone: asthenosphere. MT imaging.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Induced Polarization (IP):
options: Chargeability (mV/V). Sulfide mineral detection. Time-domain (decay curve) and frequency-domain
questions.solution: IP: metallic mineralization. Disseminated sulfides. Polarization effect at grain-fluid interface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Granite vs granodiorite: key difference:
options: Granite: K-feldspar > Plagioclase. Granodiorite: Plagioclase > K-feldspar
questions.solution: QAPF diagram: Granite = Q + A > P. Granodiorite = Q + P > A.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fluvial terrace classification: strath vs fill:
options: Strath: bedrock surface cut during incision. Fill: alluvial deposit abandoned during incision
questions.solution: Strath: planation surface. Fill: alluvial remnant. Strath = bedrock. Fill = alluvium.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Amphibole double chain structure:
options: Si₄O₁₁⁶⁻ repeating unit. Two pyroxene chains linked
questions.solution: Amphibole: double chain inosilicate. Perfect 56°/124° cleavage. OH in structure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock sample of 100 cm³ contains 25 cm³ of pore space. Its porosity is:
questions.solution: Porosity = (void volume/total volume)×100 = 25/100 = 25%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Aquiclude:
options: Impermeable unit (clay, massive shale). No significant yield
questions.solution: Aquiclude: prevents flow. Confining layer in confined aquifer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydraulic conductivity K:
options: Depends on medium + fluid. Sand: 10⁻²-10⁻⁴ m/s. Clay: <10⁻⁹ m/s
questions.solution: K: gravel (10⁻¹), sand (10⁻³), silt (10⁻⁵), clay (10⁻⁸ m/s). K = m/s or m/day.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cone of depression:
options: Drawdown cone around pumping well. Radius of influence R
questions.solution: Drawdown: s₀ = Q/(2πT)·ln(R/r_w). R increases with pumping time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pumping test specific capacity:
options: Q/s₀ = 2πT/ln(R/r_w). Q=displacement rate, s₀=drawdown at well
questions.solution: Specific capacity: Q/s₀. Indicates well productivity. Affected by well efficiency.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of zircon:
questions.solution: Zircon (ZrSiO4) — tetragonal prisms. High RI, high birefringence. Used in U-Pb dating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Pingos:
options: Ice-cored mounds in permafrost. Open-system (groundwater) and closed-system (lake)
questions.solution: Pingo: up to 100m high. Collapse pingo → thermokarst pond. Continuous permafrost zone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: NDVI (Normalized Difference Vegetation Index):
questions.solution: NDVI: >0.5 = dense vegetation. 0-0.2 = sparse. <0 = water/bare soil.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cut slope design:
options: Dip slope vs anti-dip slope. Daylighting of bedding → plane failure
questions.solution: Daylighting: bedding dips out of slope → sliding. Rock bolts, drainage, berms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Diorite:
questions.solution: Diorite: no quartz or minor quartz. More plagioclase than K-spar.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biostratigraphy:
options: Based on fossil content (biozone, assemblage zone, range zone)
questions.solution: Biozone: body fossil or trace fossil. Index fossils: ammonite zones for Mesozoic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Grain size scale (Wentworth):
options: Clay < 1/256 mm, Silt 1/256-1/16 mm, Sand 1/16-2 mm, Gravel > 2 mm
questions.solution: Wentworth: clay, silt, sand, pebble, cobble, boulder. Phi scale = -log2(mm).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metamorphic facies series: Barrovian type:
options: Medium P/T gradient (typical of continental collision). Kyanite → sillimanite
questions.solution: Barrovian: clockwise P-T path. Kyanite → sillimanite. Buchan: andalusite → sillimanite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EM induction logging:
options: Conductivity σ (mS/m). Low frequency, no borehole contact. Used in PVC-cased wells, freshwater
questions.solution: EM induction: no electrode contact. Air-filled boreholes. Freshwater vs saline aquifer.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Lateral extent of coal seam indicates:
questions.solution: Coal: formed in terrestrial swamps. Extent = stable peat-forming.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sm-Nd model age (T_DM):
options: Time when sample had same ¹⁴³Nd/¹⁴⁴Nd as depleted mantle
questions.solution: T_DM: crustal residence time. Depleted mantle: ¹⁴³Nd/¹⁴⁴Nd grows with time. Model age = when sample split.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed drops 10 m over a horizontal distance of 40 m. Its dip angle is about:
questions.solution: dip = tan⁻¹(rise/run) = tan⁻¹(10/40) ≈ 14°.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 10% moisture, 25% volatile matter and 20% ash. Its fixed carbon is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Soil water zones:
options: Saturated zone, capillary fringe, vadose zone (unsaturated). Roots in vadose
questions.solution: Vadose zone: moisture content < porosity. Infiltration, evapotranspiration, interflow.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Chlorite vs biotite in thin section:
options: Chlorite: anomalous interference colors. Biotite: strong pleochroism, bird's eye extinction
questions.solution: Chlorite: lower birefringence, green/brown. Biotite: high birefringence, strong absorption.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: River terraces — fill vs strath:
options: Fill: in alluvium. Strath: bedrock terrace with thin alluvial cover
questions.solution: Fill terrace: former floodplain now abandoned. Strath terrace: lateral planation surface.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hyperspectral remote sensing:
options: 100s of narrow bands. Spectral signature identification of minerals
questions.solution: Hyperspectral: AVIRIS, Hyperion, PRISMA, EnMAP. Mineral mapping (clay, iron, carbonate).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quick clay:
options: Thixotropic clay (high sensitivity). If disturbed, loses strength → flow failure
questions.solution: Quick clay: marine clay, leached to low salinity. Sensitivity > 30. Liquefaction flow slides.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Peridotite:
questions.solution: Peridotite: >90% mafic minerals. Source rock for basaltic magmas.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Deccan Traps — stratigraphy:
options: Formations: Jawhar, Igatpuri, Neral, Thakurwadi, Bhimashankar, Khandala, etc.
questions.solution: Deccan: ~3500m thick in Western Ghats. Twelve formations in Wai Subgroup. Intertrappean beds.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Limestone:
questions.solution: Limestone: fossiliferous, chalk, coquina, oolitic. Effervesces in HCl.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gneissic banding:
options: Alternating light (felsic) and dark (mafic) mineral layers
questions.solution: Gneiss: high grade. Segregation into bands. Compositional layering.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about the Moho is correct?
options: It separates the outer and inner core
It separates the crust from the mantle and shows a velocity increase
Seismic velocity drops sharply across it
questions.solution: The Mohorovičić discontinuity marks the crust–mantle boundary, where P-wave velocity jumps.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Banded Iron Formation (BIF):
options: Main Fe ore. Hematite + magnetite. Precambrian. Superior and Algoma types
questions.solution: BIF: iron precipitated from seawater. Oxygenation by cyanobacteria. Major Fe reserve.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A dipping coal seam outcrops over a horizontal width of 100 m on flat ground and dips at 35°. Its true thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = horizontal width × sin(dip) = 100×sin35° ≈ 57.36 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Groundwater flow equation:
questions.solution: Steady: ∂²h/∂x² + ∂²h/∂y² + ∂²h/∂z² = 0 (Laplace). Transient: diffusion equation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Soil creep indicators:
options: Tilted trees (pistol-butt), displaced walls, bent fence posts
questions.solution: Creep: slow downslope movement. Indicators: tilted trees, curved soil profiles, displaced objects.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rippability of rock:
options: Seismic velocity (Vp) determines ripping vs blasting. Vp < 1500 m/s: easy rip
questions.solution: Rippability: Caterpillar chart. Vp (m/s): easy <1500, marginal 1500-2000, hard >2000 → blast.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Siwalik Group:
options: Miocene-Pleistocene. Himalayan foreland basin. Fossils: mammals (elephant, horse, ape)
questions.solution: Siwalik: sandstone, conglomerate. Himalayan uplift record. Ramapithecus, Sivapithecus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 8 km/s and arrives 10 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 8×10 = 80 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 100 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.02. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Permafrost — active layer:
options: Seasonal thaw layer above permafrost. Depth: 0.5-5m depending on climate
questions.solution: Active layer: thaws in summer, refreezes in winter. Frost heave. Pile foundations for structures.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Joint — exfoliation (sheeting):
options: Curved joints parallel to topographic surface. Unloading (erosion)
questions.solution: Exfoliation: formation of sheet joints. Onion-like weathering. Granite domes (Half Dome).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: EM spectrum bands used:
options: Visible (0.4-0.7 μm), NIR, SWIR, TIR, Microwave (radar)
questions.solution: Multispectral: multiple bands. Hyperspectral: hundreds of narrow bands.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fold classification:
options: Anticline (upfold), Syncline (downfold). Hinge, limb, axial plane, plunge
questions.solution: Anticline: oldest in core. Syncline: youngest in core.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Thermal infrared remote sensing measures:
questions.solution: TIR: 8-14 um wavelength. Detects emitted thermal radiation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Solifluction:
options: Slow downslope flow of water-saturated soil over permafrost. Gelifluction
questions.solution: Solifluction: lobes, sheets. 1-10 cm/yr. Gelifluction: thaw of ice lenses. Terrace-like landforms.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DEM — slope and aspect:
options: Slope = max rate of change. Aspect = direction of max slope (0-360°)
questions.solution: Slope: degrees or %. Derived from DEM (3×3 window, e.g., Horn algorithm). Aspect: N, S, E, W.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about mining method selection is correct?
options: A high stripping ratio favours opencast mining
A high stripping ratio favours underground over opencast mining
questions.solution: When too much overburden must be removed per tonne (high stripping ratio), underground mining is preferred.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 10000 m² has 30 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 10000×30 = 300000 m³ = 0.3 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Heavy mineral separation for provenance:
options: Bromoform (SG=2.89) separates light from heavy minerals
questions.solution: Heavy minerals: SG>2.89. Zircon, tourmaline, rutile (ultra-stable). Garnet, staurolite, kyanite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cooper-Jacob straight-line method: drawdown s vs log(t) plot slope:
questions.solution: Cooper-Jacob: valid for small u (<0.01). Slope Δs over one log cycle = 2.3Q/(4πT).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 8% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+20+8) = 68%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Strike-slip fault:
questions.solution: Strike-slip: San Andreas Fault. Transform plate boundary.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of garnet:
questions.solution: Garnet: dodecahedral or trapezohedral crystals. Used as abrasive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer (K = 10 m/day) of cross-section 500 m² has a hydraulic gradient of 0.01. The groundwater discharge is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tourmaline chemical formula:
questions.solution: Tourmaline: complex borosilicate. Wide solid solution. Schorl (Fe), elbaite (Li,Na), dravite (Mg).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: P-T path determination from garnet zoning and inclusions:
options: Garnet core = early P-T. Rim = peak conditions. Inclusions record path
questions.solution: Garnet: chronometer. Inclusion assemblages at different distances from core = different P-T. Thermobarometry.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Boudinage:
options: Boudin (barrel-shaped) structure in competent layer stretched in incompetent matrix
questions.solution: Boudinage: competent layer (quartzite) boudins in incompetent matrix (schist). Necking.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 4% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (4+20+15) = 61%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biotite belongs to:
questions.solution: Biotite — black mica. One perfect basal cleavage. Common in granite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hydrological modeling in GIS:
options: Flow accumulation, stream network, watershed delineation. Sink filling required
questions.solution: DEM → fill sinks → flow direction (D8) → flow accumulation → stream threshold → watershed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 10% moisture, 20% volatile matter and 10% ash. Its fixed carbon is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal sample (proximate analysis) has 8% moisture, 30% volatile matter and 15% ash. Its fixed carbon is:
questions.solution: Fixed C = 100 − (moisture + VM + ash) = 100 − (8+30+15) = 47%.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mylonite:
options: Fine-grained, foliated rock in ductile shear zone. Grain size reduction by dynamic recrystallization
questions.solution: Mylonite: ductile deformation. Porphyroclasts in fine matrix. S-C fabrics. Protomylonite→mylonite→ultramylonite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 10000 m² has 50 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 10000×50 = 500000 m³ = 0.5 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 5 m³ of coal, 45 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 45/5 = 9 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Facies model — fluvial meandering:
options: Channel (lag + lateral accretion) + floodplain (levee + crevasse splay + swamp). Vertically fining
questions.solution: Meandering: point bar (epsilon cross-bedding). Lateral accretion surfaces (LAS). Mud plug.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 50 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.05. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Coastal — barrier island:
options: Long, narrow sand island parallel to coast. Lagoon behind. Inlet cuts
questions.solution: Barrier: wave-dominated. Dune ridge, beach, washover fans. Sea-level rise → barrier transgression.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Foliation (planar fabric) is a feature of which rock type?
questions.solution: Directed pressure aligns minerals into foliation in metamorphic rocks like schist and gneiss.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Specific gravity of quartz:
questions.solution: Quartz SG ~2.65. Heavy minerals have SG > 2.85.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A buried erosion surface separating older from much younger rocks is a(n):
questions.solution: An unconformity represents a gap (missing time) in the rock record.
